user_message: I need the team view on migration architecture. I think there's an opportunity here but need to validate.

input_artifacts.content:
From: Brian Yamamoto <brian.yamamoto@alpineskihou.com>
To: Marcus Williams <marcus.williams@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-03-16 15:42 EST
Subject: Re: Re: migration architecture Timeline

Just checking in on the migration architecture proposal. Our COO is asking for a status update before the board meeting next week.

---
From: Marcus Williams <marcus.williams@microsoft.com>
To: Brian Yamamoto <brian.yamamoto@alpineskihou.com>
Date: 2024-02-21 16:34 EST
Subject: migration architecture - Action Required

Just checking in on the migration architecture proposal. Our COO is asking for a status update before the board meeting next week.

---
From: Brian Yamamoto <brian.yamamoto@alpineskihou.com>
To: Marcus Williams <marcus.williams@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-01-24 14:11 EST
Subject: Follow-up: migration architecture Review

Thank you for the discussion last week. We'd like to move forward with migration architecture. Can we schedule a call this week to finalize the timeline?

Meeting: migration architecture Discussion - Alpine Ski House
Date: 2024-01-19
Duration: 45 minutes
Attendees: Brian Yamamoto (Alpine Ski House), Marcus Williams (Microsoft)

[00:01:15] Brian Yamamoto (Alpine Ski House): Appreciate everyone making time. The main agenda today is migration architecture.

[00:07:00] Brian Yamamoto (Alpine Ski House): The timeline for security compliance is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

[00:09:00] Marcus Williams (Microsoft): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is technical validation.

[00:14:00] Brian Yamamoto (Alpine Ski House): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is resource allocation.

[00:19:00] Marcus Williams (Microsoft): Our architect reviewed this and has concerns about vendor dependencies. Can we address those?

[00:21:00] Brian Yamamoto (Alpine Ski House): Our architect reviewed this and has concerns about timeline constraints. Can we address those?

[00:24:00] Marcus Williams (Microsoft): On the migration architecture front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around resource allocation.

[00:27:00] Marcus Williams (Microsoft): On the migration architecture front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around stakeholder alignment.

[00:30:00] Marcus Williams (Microsoft): Our VP reviewed this and has concerns about integration complexity. Can we address those?

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF11-M trap (Brief format doesn't natively differentiate first-meeting vs recurring); produces complete One-page brief with attendee context, account snapshot, talking points, likely objections, open item
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF11-M failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF11-M failure mode (Brief format doesn't natively differentiate first-meeting vs recurring)
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct migration architecture decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF11-M -- Brief format doesn't natively differentiate first-meeting vs recurring
Produce structural verdict on whether current routing adequately serves this scenario
